title: Build an E-Commerce Product Listing
description: Create a filterable, sortable, searchable product catalog with a responsive grid — the foundation of every e-commerce browse page.
requirements: Fetch or load a product dataset with id, name, price, category, and image
Category filter dropdown or chip buttons
Price range filter with min and max inputs
Search input filtering by product name (client-side or debounced)
Sort controls for price and name (ascending/descending)
Responsive product grid using CSS Grid or Flexbox
Stable keys using product.id — never array index
Empty state when filters match no products
acceptanceCriteria: Price filter 100–500 excludes products outside range
Sort by price low-to-high orders correctly
Changing parent filter state does not re-render unchanged ProductCards unnecessarily (verify with React DevTools)
npm run build passes with strict TypeScript
hints: Derive filteredProducts with useMemo from products + filter state — do not mutate original array.
Chain: products.filter(...).filter(...).sort(...) or pipe through helper functions.
Extract ProductCard as memoized component; pass primitive props where possible.
Keep sort state as 'price-asc' | 'price-desc' | 'name-asc' enum string.
Use Fake Store API or a static JSON file for product data.

debuggingScenarios.title: Wrong keys
debuggingScenarios.symptom: Input focus lost in list item, or wrong item updates after sort
debuggingScenarios.cause: Using array index as key when list order changes
debuggingScenarios.fix: Use product.id as key — stable unique identifier

debuggingScenarios.title: Unnecessary re-renders
debuggingScenarios.symptom: All cards flash in React DevTools when one filter changes
debuggingScenarios.cause: Inline arrow functions or object props passed to every ProductCard
debuggingScenarios.fix: React.memo on ProductCard; useMemo for filtered list; stable callbacks with useCallback if needed

debuggingScenarios.title: Filter bugs
debuggingScenarios.symptom: Price filter shows wrong products or mutates original list
debuggingScenarios.cause: Mutating products array with .sort() in place or wrong comparison operators
debuggingScenarios.fix: Spread or slice before sort: [...filtered].sort(); use >= and <= for price range
